ip_outcome_note: Card name directly names the depicted character. Confirmed via Bandai official listing, Son Goku: Childhood character tag, Saiyan/World Tournament traits, and the monkey tail visual identifier in the art (present only in pre-Saiyan-Saga Goku depictions). Frontmatter updated: ip_verified: true.

trivia_bullets.text: TB2-051 is from World Martial Arts Tournament (DBS-TB02), released September 21, 2018. Yellow cards in TB2 represent the 21st World Tournament era. TB2-051 Link partner is Unyielding Victory Jackie Chun (SPR). The official Bandai SR/SPR strategy page: 'We recreated the scene from the manga where Jackie Chun and Goku fought through a skill! Double Strike and Triple Attack make a devastating combo.'
sources: dbs-cardgame.com: World Martial Arts Tournament Design Concept strategy page
dbs-cardgame.com: TB02 SR/SPR Cards Showdown strategy page

trivia_bullets.text: Yellow Battle card, Super Rare (SR and SPR parallel), power 15,000, energy cost 3 Yellow, Combo Power 5,000. Character tag: Son Goku: Childhood. Traits: Saiyan/World Tournament. Activate:Main (once per turn): choose 1 Unyielding Victory Jackie Chun in Battle Area and switch to Rest Mode; if Leader is a yellow World Tournament, this card gains +5,000 power, Double Strike, and Triple Attack for the turn. Full combo — 20,000 power, two damage, triple attack — activates only when the Jackie Chun partner is sacrificed.

The 21st World Martial Arts Tournament (Dragon Ball anime episodes 14-28) is the first tournament arc. Young Goku reaches the final against Jackie Chun (Master Roshi in disguise — a wig and sunglasses, entered secretly to defeat his own students and keep them motivated to train). The match ends with Jackie Chun narrowly winning after Goku transforms into a Great Ape (Roshi blasts away the moon) — determined by martial arts experience rather than raw power. TB2-051's mechanic literally replays the match: Goku draws power from Jackie Chun's 'sacrifice' (Rest Mode) to deliver the finishing Triple Attack.
sources: Wikipedia: World Martial Arts Tournament (Dragon Ball)
CBR: 'Dragon Ball Episode 28 Brought the Anime's First Tournament to an Unforgettable Conclusion'

trivia_bullets.text: TB2-051 anchors the childhood / original Dragon Ball phase of the Son Goku character node. The monkey tail visible in the Vision section is the canonical pre-Z visual marker: Goku's tail was removed during the Saiyan Saga (anime: by Kami; manga: by Vegeta), so its presence confirms a pre-Z depiction. This phase is distinct from adult Goku prints across BT1 and BT2.
